steps.title: Factor the denominator
steps.content: The denominator $6x + 42$ has a greatest common factor of 6: $$6x + 42 = 6(x + 7)$$

steps.title: Simplify and find b
steps.content: Substitute the factored form and simplify: $$\frac{24}{6(x+7)} = \frac{4}{x+7}$$ by dividing numerator and denominator by 6. Comparing to $\frac{4}{x+b}$, we have $x + 7 = x + b$, so $b = 7$. That confirms A is correct.
